entities.name: SWEET SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop known for storing sugar in its stems.

entities.name: GRAIN SORGHUM
entities.description: A sorghum variety used as a source for the Stay green1 locus.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per plant or area.

entities.name: STEM SUGAR ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The process of storing sugar within the stem of the plant.

entities.name: SUGAR REMOBILIZATION
entities.description: The movement of sugars from vegetative tissues to reproductive tissues.

entities.name: REDUCING SUGAR CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of reducing sugars in the plant stem.

entities.name: STAY GREEN1
entities.description: A locus associated with delayed senescence and higher sugar accumulation.

entities.name: ABA RESPONSE FACTORS
entities.description: Transcription factors that mediate responses to abscisic acid.

entities.name: INVERTASE GENES
entities.description: Genes encoding enzymes that catalyze the hydrolysis of sucrose.

entities.name: SUCROSE TRANSPORTER GENES
entities.description: Genes encoding proteins that facilitate the transport of sucrose.

entities.name: ABA-RESPONSIVE ELEMENTS
entities.description: DNA sequences that bind ABA-responsive transcription factors.

entities.name: SUGAR SIGNALING MOTIFS
entities.description: DNA sequences involved in the regulation of genes by sugar signaling.

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISM
entities.description: A variation in a single nucleotide that can affect gene regulation.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental condition characterized by lack of water availability.

entities.name: ABSCISIC ACID
entities.description: A phytohormone that plays a key role in plant stress responses.

entities.name: ABA SIGNALING
entities.description: The signal transduction pathway initiated by abscisic acid.

entities.name: SUGAR SIGNALING
entities.description: The process by which plants sense and respond to sugar levels.

entities.name: INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The transfer of genetic material from one species or variety to another.

entities.name: STEM
entities.description: The vegetative part of the plant where sugar is stored.

entities.name: PEDUNCLE
entities.description: The stalk supporting the inflorescence.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE TISSUES
entities.description: Tissues involved in the production of seeds and grains.

relationships.description: regulates the process of moving sugars to reproductive sinks
induces the activation of the ABA signaling pathway
triggers the movement of sugars from stems to grains
augments the final grain yield of the crop
leads to higher accumulation of sugar in the stems
contains genes that exhibit differential regulation
regulates the expression of these factors
regulates the expression of genes involved in sugar breakdown
regulates the expression of genes involved in sugar transport
serve as binding sites for the ABA signaling pathway
serve as regulatory elements for sugar signaling
involved in the regulation of sugar movement
accounts for the differential regulation of ABF genes
undergoes sugar remobilization under drought stress
causes a decrease in reducing sugar content in the stem
